name: Hiding
description: A defensive state where the player enters a hiding spot or concealed posture to avoid detection, searches, or chase pressure.
design_notes: Hiding should preserve fear while keeping player agency. A good hiding spot has enter and exit rules, readable monster search behavior, detection risks, animation lock boundaries, and reasons to leave. If hiding is always safe, tension stalls; if it is opaque, players feel cheated.
design_purpose: Give players a non-combat survival option against stronger threats.
Create close-range suspense through line of sight, noise, and monster search patterns.
Support level design that alternates exposure, pursuit, concealment, and escape.
player_fantasy: The player feels barely concealed while a threat searches nearby and every sound could give them away.

parameters.name: hiding_spot_id
parameters.description: Interactable concealment point used for state and AI search.

parameters.name: hiding_state
parameters.description: Current player concealment state and animation gate.

parameters.name: enter_exit_window_ms
parameters.description: Animation lock window for entering or leaving the spot.

parameters.name: line_of_sight_grace_ms
parameters.description: Brief allowance before a monster confirms the hiding transition.

parameters.name: search_suspicion_threshold
parameters.description: AI suspicion band that causes the monster to inspect the spot.

parameters.name: exit_position_rule_id
parameters.description: Placement rule used when the player leaves hiding.

edge_cases: The monster sees the player during the enter animation and must decide whether hiding succeeds.
The exit socket is blocked by the monster, another player, or a moved physics object.
A scripted scare or checkpoint restore occurs while the player is inside a hiding spot.
Multiple players or companions try to use the same hiding_spot_id.
The player makes noise while hidden and the monster should inspect without instantly knowing the exact location.
common_bugs: AI line-of-sight ignores hiding_state and detects the player through the hiding mesh.
The camera clips outside lockers or under beds during enter and peek transitions.
Exit placement pushes the player into a wall or directly into monster collision.
Hiding spots never release occupancy after interrupted animations or reload.
Search suspicion resets too quickly and lets players exploit the same spot repeatedly.
balancing_notes: Safe hiding spots create relief; searchable hiding spots preserve tension but need readable telegraphs.
Long entry animations increase commitment and fear, but excessive lockout can feel unfair during chase sequences.
Repeated use should become riskier through monster memory, noise, or search patterns rather than arbitrary failure.
accessibility_notes: Support toggle or hold options for breathing, peeking, or staying hidden interactions.
Use subtitles or directional indicators for monster proximity cues that affect hiding decisions.
Avoid pitch-black hiding views without alternate readability settings.

implementation_notes.unity: Represent hiding_spot_id as an interactable with occupancy and exit sockets. On enter, set hiding_state, disable or remap movement, notify AI perception with the last visible position, and only mark hidden after line_of_sight_grace_ms and animation confirmation.
implementation_notes.godot: Use an Area2D or Area3D for the hiding spot and a player state machine for entering, hidden, peeking, and exiting. Store occupancy and exit_position_rule_id so reloads and interrupted animations can clear or restore the spot safely.
implementation_notes.unreal: Implement hiding spots as Actors with interaction components, camera targets, and replicated occupancy if needed. AI should transition from chase to search using last_known_player_position and inspect hiding_spot_id based on suspicion rather than raw visibility alone.
implementation_notes.web: In Phaser or custom engines, separate the hide interaction transaction from the rendered sprite or camera mask. Keep hiding_state, occupancy, and exit socket validation in gameplay state so animation skips cannot bypass detection rules.

example_games.title: Alien: Isolation
example_games.note: Uses hiding places as part of monster avoidance.

example_games.title: Outlast
example_games.note: Uses lockers and beds as hiding options during pursuit.
